structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Divergent-convergent graph structure with an iterative feedback loop. It branches into 3 paths from N1, converges at N3/N4, and can loop back from N4 through N5 into three distinct parallel/alternative feedback entry points (N2A/B/C) before converging again.

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure begins at START and transitions to N1, where a single state is expanded into three distinct paths via pass-through states N1a, N1b, and N1c into states N2A, N2B, and N2C respectively. These three paths converge at N3, which adds a differentiating input. The sequence transitions to N4, where the path fit is re-sorted based on the new input. From N4, three outcomes are possible: if a threshold is not met due to weak separation, the process transitions to N5 to reopen continuation, sending the execution back through pass-through states (N5a, N5b, N5c) to N2A, N2B, and N2C to repeat the evaluation loop. If one path is selected because it remains strongest, execution transitions to N6, which closes the graph. If continuation is not justified, execution transitions directly to END.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: START -> N1 -> [N1a->N2A, N1b->N2B, N1c->N2C] -> N3 -> N4. From N4: IF [threshold not met] -> N5 -> [N5a->N2A, N5b->N2B, N5c->N2C] (Loop); IF [one path selected] -> N6 (Close Graph); IF [continuation not justified] -> END.

plausible_interpretations: The paths are processed concurrently or tracking multiple parallel hypotheses.
The paths are mutually exclusive alternative routes chosen based on unstated conditions.
